creator: garrigue@math.nagoya-u.ac.jp
text:
If you mean that you intend to skip 8.2pl1 and wait for 8.3, then I beg to =
 disagree.
 
 8.2 and 8.2pl1 are not compatible, and many proofs will not go through usin=
 g 8.2.
 For now more than 1 year, 8.2pl1 has been the standard version, meaning tha=
 t most proofs exchanged require it.
 Even with the introduction of 8.3, there will be a transition period, so in=
  an ideal world there should be coq and coq-devel.
 
 My experience was installing two versions of the coq package, and then taki=
 ng 2 hours to compile the port, until I understood that only a deprecated v=
 ersion was available from the ports, under an incorrect version number.
 
 Anyway, thanks for providing a port.
 
 Jacques
 
 From: Johan van Selst <johans@stack.nl>
 
 Jacques Garrigue wrote:
 > The Coq port in both 8.0 and 8.1-RC2 is labelled as version 8.2.1, but
 > it is actually version 8.2. This is most confusing since the current
 > release version, for more than 1 year, is 8.2pl1, which has some
 > incompatibilities.
 
 I am aware of this problem and plan to fix it with the next update.
 But I don't think it is important/confusing enough to change it right
 away and let all coq users re-install this program for no good reason.
 
 
 Regards,
 Johan
